Honda, DNMX, DNJP, and DIAM root cause analysis status update meeting.  
Peeling bubbles were generated at the contact portion between the pin and the glass, the contact resistance became high resistance, and display failure occurred.
Warranty occurrence estimated to be lot dependent based on resin application and UV hardening time.
Countermeasure:
1)	DNMX Quality Alert to inform Associates of quality defect [Implemented]
2)	DNMX Suspect LCD Lot segregate [Implemented]
3)	DNMX add 1hr burn-in without CAN (1hr w/o CAN + 1 hr w/ CAN) [Implemented]
4)	DNMX “tapping” during burn-in [9/21/2020]
5)	DNMX Increase burn-in with CAN from 1 hr to 2hr [10/15/2020]
6)	DNMX LCD Assembly Jig to eliminate pin stress during install to Meter [10/312020]
7)	Kyocera UV irradiation control improvement [August/2020]
8)	Kyocera Lighting inspection at low current to detect abnormal pin resistance [August/2020]
9)	Kyocera UV coating inspection – 1st and last per lot [9/21/2020]
10)	Kyocera LCD backside resin application [under investigation]
11)	Kyocera UV coating amount increase (pressure 0.4 ? 0.45Mpa) [under investigation]

Subject: Crank Sensor
Progress Updates: VTC Solenoid Valve NG.  The internal cup that is used to actuate the valve was stamped incorreclty by the tier two supplier.  The incorrect stamping process causes the part to stick which delays eVTC system response time.  The strart time of this issue cooinsids with a 4-M change that was made a the tier two supplier.
Countermeasure:
Decherd Adoption:  Decherd has implemented a 100% firing test with modified test logic that is programmed to reject any engine with slow eVTC response time.   The part was changed from the current level NG part to previous level OK part.  Positive hot test results confrim that this part change was effective.
The tier two supplier has taken numberous actions to prevent this issue from reocurring.  -
Asian has implemented new testing in their facilty that will preven the outflow of defective parts.
